pi_items.source: Web search and chemical database verification: Sodium (CAS 7440-23-5) is a highly reactive alkali metal that reacts vigorously with water, liberating flammable hydrogen gas and generating heat.
Chemical literature confirms sodium water reaction: Na + H2O → NaOH + H2 (hydrogen gas). Sodium floats on water while reacting and produces hydrogen gas.
Web search confirms calcium carbide (CAS 75-20-7) is hazard Class 4.3 (Water Reactive/Dangerous When Wet). It is highly water-reactive, classified as flammable and water reactive.

Chemical reaction: CaC2 + 2H2O → C2H2 (acetylene) + Ca(OH)2. Calcium carbide produces acetylene gas which is highly flammable and may ignite spontaneously. 1kg of pure calcium carbide produces 366L of acetylene.

Web search and chemical literature: Acetone (CAS 67-64-1) is a polar aprotic solvent that is miscible with water and does NOT react with water. Acetone can be prepared in aqueous solutions without adverse reaction.

Web search confirms potassium (CAS 7440-09-7) reacts explosively with water to form potassium hydroxide. The reaction is so violent and dangerous that potassium must be kept away from any contact with water.

Chemical literature: Potassium reacts with water to produce potassium hydroxide and hydrogen gas. The heat generated generally ignites the hydrogen formed and can initiate combustion of the potassium metal itself.

Web search confirms ethanol (CAS 64-17-5) shows no reaction with water. Ethanol is miscible with water and is a common solvent used in aqueous solutions. No hazardous chemical reaction occurs when ethanol contacts water.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims about chemical water reactivity have been independently verified. Sodium and Potassium are highly water-reactive alkali metals that produce flammable hydrogen gas. Calcium Carbide is water-reactive and produces acetylene gas. Acetone and Ethanol are both non-reactive with water and are miscible with it. The CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/) is a real NOAA database containing hazardous chemical information including water reactivity data.
